codeReview.summary: Reviewed the original implementation plus the two fix commits. The fixes now emit one `SplitFace` per outer trimming loop and assign inner loops only to the smallest containing outer fragment, which addresses the original blocking provenance bugs.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `rust-bop-worker` skill still requires a clean `cargo clippy -p truck-bop` run even though the mission guidance and services file explicitly document that clippy is intentionally skipped because of known pre-existing failures outside `truck-bop`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Volumes/DPC/work/cad/truck-new/.factory/skills/rust-bop-worker/SKILL.md` step 4 says to run and address `cargo clippy -p truck-bop`; `/Users/dongpengcheng/.factory/missions/28558208-113c-4faf-9654-e1aaded1d9ab/AGENTS.md` says clippy may fail due to pre-existing issues, and `/Volumes/DPC/work/cad/truck-new/.factory/services.yaml` sets `lint` to `echo "Skipping clippy due to known pre-existing issues in other workspace crates"`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The project library still describes face splitting only at a high level and does not capture the fragment-ownership invariant that each outer loop becomes its own `SplitFace` and inner loops are assigned by UV containment to the smallest containing outer region.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Volumes/DPC/work/cad/truck-new/.factory/library/architecture.md` only says `Face Splitting: UV projection, trimming loops`, while the fixed logic in `truck-bop/src/trim.rs:35` and `truck-bop/src/trim.rs:268` now depends on per-outer partitioning plus containment-based hole ownership.

summary: Re-reviewed `split-face-records` against the original failing implementation and the two fix features. The combined fixes in `bb2bb642` and `920c3dae` resolve the prior blocking issues: fragments are partitioned per outer loop, holes are assigned by containment, and the updated regression tests cover both multiple outer regions and nested containment ownership.
